message:
fix(commands): ZADD GT/LT/NX error unified + ZMPOP/BZMPOP nested-pair shape

Two Redis-7 wire-parity fixes on the ZSET side, plus the matching
conformance-fixture goldens.

crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs
  * ZADD flag conflict: collapse the two separate "GT and LT" /
    "GT, LT, and NX" errors into a single branch emitting
    "ERR GT, LT, and/or NX options at the same time are not compatible"
    — the exact string Redis 7 uses for any flag-combo conflict
    involving GT/LT/NX. Previously we split the wording, so a
    client asserting on Redis's exact string failed depending on
    which two flags the caller combined.
  * ZMPOP / BZMPOP reply shape: each popped (member, score) pair
    is now wrapped in `RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![member, score]))`
    before being pushed onto the outer array. Redis 7 returns a
    flat outer Array containing per-pop `[member, score]` inner
    arrays, not a flat bulk-string sequence. Previously we pushed
    the BulkStrings directly, which flattened the shape and broke
    any client that indexed on the inner arrays.
  * Matching fix in the `collect_popped` helper branches:
    `flat_map` over (member, score) pairs rewrites to `map` that
    yields `RespFrame::Array(Some(vec![...]))` per pair. Keeps
    the pop-count behavior identical but produces the nested
    shape Redis returns.
  * New `#[cfg(test)] fn zadd_conflict_errors_match_redis_wording`
    asserts the unified error string is emitted for all three
    combos: GT+LT, NX+GT, NX+LT.

crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_zset.json
  * Updates three cases to the unified "GT, LT, and/or NX" wording:
      - zadd_gt_lt_conflict     (GT+LT)
      - zadd_nx_gt_conflict     (NX+GT)
      - zadd_nx_lt_conflict     (NX+LT)

crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_blocking.json
  * Updates two BZMPOP / BZPOPMIN-style goldens to expect the
    nested `[[member, score]]` array shape. Previously the goldens
    expected a flat `[member, score]` pair — which matched our
    old wrong reply, not Redis. Two fixture entries rewired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 struct VendoredRedisOracle {
     child: Child,
     port: u16,
@@ -2719,6 +2870,50 @@ fn core_sort_live_redis_matches_runtime() {
     assert!(report.failed.is_empty());
 }
 
+#[test]
+fn core_copy_live_redis_matches_runtime() {
+    let cfg = HarnessConfig::default_paths();
+    let oracle_server = VendoredRedisOracle::start(&cfg);
+    let oracle = LiveOracleConfig {
+        host: "127.0.0.1".to_string(),
+        port: oracle_server.port,
+        ..LiveOracleConfig::default()
+    };
+    let report =
+        run_live_redis_diff_for_cases(&cfg, "core_copy.json", CORE_COPY_LIVE_STABLE_CASES, &oracle)
+            .expect("copy live diff");
+    assert_eq!(
+        report.total, report.passed,
+        "mismatches: {:?}",
+        report.failed
+    );
+    assert!(report.failed.is_empty());
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn core_connection_live_redis_matches_runtime() {
+    let cfg = HarnessConfig::default_paths();
+    let oracle_server = VendoredRedisOracle::start(&cfg);
+    let oracle = LiveOracleConfig {
+        host: "127.0.0.1".to_string(),
+        port: oracle_server.port,
+        ..LiveOracleConfig::default()
+    };
+    let report = run_live_redis_diff_for_cases(
+        &cfg,
+        "core_connection.json",
+        CORE_CONNECTION_LIVE_STABLE_CASES,
+        &oracle,
+    )
+    .expect("connection live diff");
+    assert_eq!(
+        report.total, report.passed,
+        "mismatches: {:?}",
+        report.failed
+    );
+    assert!(report.failed.is_empty());
+}
+
